+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The current connection tree search functionality does not include child connections when their parent folder matches the search criteria, leading to an incomplete display of search results.\nApproach: Refactor `ConnectionTreeSearchTextFilter` to pre-calculate a set of all `ConnectionInfo` objects that should be visible based on the filter text. This set will include: 1) objects directly matching the filter criteria; 2) all descendants of a matching `ContainerInfo`; and 3) all ancestors of any matching `ConnectionInfo` or `ContainerInfo`. The `Filter` method will then simply check for presence in this pre-calculated set."
 }

diff --git a/.project-roadmap/issues-db/upstream/2195.json b/.project-roadmap/issues-db/upstream/2195.json

   "body_snippet": "It is possible to craft a connection that executes arbitrary PowerShell commands when it is double-clicked. This is problematic because an attacker could export a code-executing connection and then trick someone into importing and clicking on it, allowing the attacker to execute code on their machine. The root of the issue is this piece of code that is used for making PowerShell connections:\r\n\r\n![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29503289/162325436-c75e93dc-7ab3-4e49-b30d-4ccf73dc...",
   "our_status": "testing",
   "priority": "P1-security",
@@ -92,17 +92,21 @@
       "is_ours": true,
       "analyzed": false,
       "action_needed": false
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM7rA2Ms",
+      "author": "robertpopa22",
+      "date": "2026-02-23T06:04:46Z",
+      "snippet": "**Fix available for testing**\n\n**Commit:** **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**\n**Branch:** `main`\n**What changed:** Conduct a detailed security review of how deserialized `Conn\n\n**Download latest beta:** **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**\n\nPlease test and report if this resolves your issue.\n\n---\n_Automated by mRemoteNG Issue Intelligence System_",
+      "is_ours": true,
+      "analyzed": false,
+      "action_needed": false
     }
   ],
-  "comments_cursor": "2026-02-21T10:23:24Z",
+  "comments_cursor": "2026-02-23T06:04:54Z",
   "unread_comments": 5,
   "needs_action": true,
   "waiting_for_us": false,
-  "last_synced": "2026-02-21T12:06:48Z",
-  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): While `SecureXmlHelper` effectively prevents XXE attacks during XML deserialization, a comprehensive audit is required to confirm that no other forms of code execution are possible through the subsequent use of deserialized data, especially in fields that may trigger external commands.\nApproach: Conduct a detailed security review of how deserialized `ConnectionInfo` properties (especially those related to external commands or scripting) are handled to identify and mitigate any command injection or arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities.",
-  "token_usage": {
-    "input_tokens": 11,
-    "output_tokens": 6048,
-    "cost_usd": 0.585818
-  }
+  "last_synced": "2026-02-23T10:20:21Z",
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): While `SecureXmlHelper` effectively prevents XXE attacks during XML deserialization, a comprehensive audit is required to confirm that no other forms of code execution are possible through the subsequent use of deserialized data, especially in fields that may trigger external commands.\nApproach: Conduct a detailed security review of how deserialized `ConnectionInfo` properties (especially those related to external commands or scripting) are handled to identify and mitigate any command injection or arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities."
 }

   "body_snippet": "1.78.2.3228\n\nWith external tool, if password contains a comma, the comma acts as a divider and the variable get split.\nIf I have a password 123456789, the comma is just omitted, if I have a password 1234,56789 the external tool receives two strings, divided in two, one 1234 and the other 56789\nTo test it, create a .cmd file that echoes the %Password% 1234,56789\n```\n@echo off\necho \"%1%\"\necho \"%2%\"\npause\n```\nand pass %Password% to it, the `echo \"%2%\"` should give a `\"\" ` result if working normally...",
